bookdetHome.title: BookDet is being brought into the same admin experience as Arbeidskassen
bookdetHome.description: This first version prioritizes a shared admin surface with the same navbar, theme, and dashboard language as the rest of the platform. Customer-facing pages and dedicated domains like bookdet.no will come in a separate phase.

status.shellDetail: BookDet now follows the same design system and global navigation pattern.
status.domainDetail: Customer-facing booking surfaces and dedicated domains are intentionally kept separate from the admin flow.
status.phaseDetail: Calendar, resources, and availability are the next iteration for the module.

cards.adminDescription: The module now fits into the same visual system as Arbeidskassen, with shared top navigation and a consistent admin feel.
cards.customerDescription: BookDet will also support customer-facing booking pages and standalone domains once that surface is ready.
cards.workflowDescription: The next milestone is an operational UI for appointments, availability, and resource management in true Arbeidskassen style.

milestones.oneDescription: Establish a clear admin entry point for BookDet using the same global navigation as the rest of the platform.
milestones.twoDescription: Build the calendar overview, booking queue, and resource administration inside the module admin surface.
milestones.threeDescription: Separate the public booking experience for custom domains, branding, and customer-friendly scheduling pages.

searchBook.title: Search and book resources
searchBook.description: This page is intentionally styled as a bright and spacious entry point for the customer-facing booking flow, while staying inside the shared BookDet shell for now.

one.label: Experience
one.detail: An Airbnb-style surface for search, availability, and clear decisions.

two.label: Purpose
two.detail: This area is ready to surface time slots, resources, and pricing in one calm flow.

three.label: Status
three.detail: The navigation is now in place and ready for real booking data next.

myBookings.title: My bookings
myBookings.description: This surface is reserved for a clean self-service overview of upcoming and past bookings, using the same storefront-style calmness as the search flow.
myBookings.panelTitle: What the user should be able to do

one.label: Overview
one.detail: Quick access to date, place, status, and any important changes.

two.label: Self-service
two.detail: A calm customer-facing surface for small updates without admin noise.

three.label: Guidance
three.detail: Confirmations, checklists, and reminders can live here later.

checklist.one: See the status of upcoming bookings

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Get a quick read on resources, incoming bookings, and what needs attention today.

utilization.title: Utilization next 7 days
utilization.label: Occupancy rate
utilization.detail: Demand is strongest in the afternoon and early evening. Meeting Room A and the sound kit are the busiest right now.

nextStep.title: Recommended next steps
nextStep.description: This first admin iteration is ready for operational use, and these are the most natural improvements to build next:
nextStep.one: Connect the queue to real booking requests and dates.
nextStep.two: Expand the resource flow with more rules, availability, and imagery.
nextStep.three: Let checklists follow the booking through the full handoff flow.

bookings.title: Bookings
bookings.description: Track incoming requests, confirmations, and manual clarifications in one shared queue.
bookings.tableDescription: Shows the most relevant bookings for the admin team right now.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: Manage rooms, vehicles, and equipment in a calmer, more operational admin view.

stats.bookableTodayDetail: Can be booked without extra review
stats.maintenanceDetail: Needs follow-up before the next booking

resources.tableDescription: Status, visibility, and quick actions for the most important resources right now.

new.title: Create resource
new.description: Start by choosing the category and giving the resource a clear name.
new.question: What kind of resource?
new.help: Choose the best-fit category before moving on to settings and rules.

detail.publishedDescription: The resource is visible on Bookdet and can be booked by people with access.
detail.descriptionValue: Here is a description of the resource, with the most important details users should know before booking.
detail.uploadPlaceholder: Drag and drop or click to upload more images.

checklists.title: Checklists
checklists.description: Build operational checklists that can follow the booking before, during, and after use.
checklists.builderDescription: A first setup for handoff and quality control linked to the resource.
checklists.sidebarDescription: This page is now set up as a lightweight builder for checkpoints, field types, and required documentation.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Keep booking rules, copy, and module defaults in one place.

general.title: Core rules
general.description: Define duration, cancellation windows, and how much should require manual approval.

portal.title: Booking portal
portal.description: Give the customer-facing flow the right tone, guidance, and expectations.

sidebar.title: Status
sidebar.description: The next natural step is to connect these settings to real rules, calendar logic, and notifications.

teamareaHome.title: A calmer place for updates, announcements, and milestones
teamareaHome.description: The first version of TeamArea brings together quick status updates, important announcements, and progress across the organization and departments in one clean feed.

composer.prompt: Share an update, milestone, or important note with the team...

teamareaHome.emptyState: No posts match this view yet.

posts.launchTitle: We launched the partner portal today 🚀
posts.launchBody: Huge thanks to the design and operations teams for a clean final sprint. We will do an internal demo tomorrow at 09:30 and collect feedback directly in TeamArea.
posts.announcementTitle: New routine for weekly department updates
posts.announcementBody: Starting next week, we will use TeamArea for short Monday updates per department. The goal is fewer status meetings and smoother day-to-day communication.
posts.opsTitle: The operations team has Friday covered ✅
posts.opsBody: Staffing is now confirmed for Friday. If anyone needs a swap, post it before 14:00 so we can handle it in the same flow.

todayHome.title: Today will become the operational module for tasks, planning, and day-to-day flow
todayHome.description: The first version establishes Today inside the same admin universe as Arbeidskassen, with shared navigation, the same design system, and a clear place in the platform. The actual workflow for shifts, tasks, and internal coordination will be built out here next.

status.shellDetail: Today starts with the same global structure as the rest of the platform.
status.scopeDetail: Tasks, shift planning, and team flow are gathered in one module.
status.phaseDetail: The first working surfaces will be built as planning and overview tools.

cards.planningDescription: Today is meant to become the home for daily tasks, priorities, and operational visibility across the team.
cards.collaborationDescription: The module is intended to connect tasks, availability, and ownership throughout the day.
cards.platformDescription: Today is being built as a natural extension of Arbeidskassen, not as a separate admin world.

milestones.oneDescription: Create Today as its own module app with the same global navigation and visual system as Arbeidskassen.
milestones.twoDescription: Build the first overview for tasks, availability, and planning across teams and departments.
milestones.threeDescription: Develop more advanced flows for shifts, progress tracking, and internal coordination in daily operations.
